MELItem.Title: Portable Oxygen Bottles or Units (Including Masks and Hoses)

ListItem.Para: One operative unit is available for each required cabin crew.

An inoperative or not properly serviced portable oxygen bottle/unit remains in a certified location until removed or serviced at the next suitable maintenance facility. Location placarding is removed or obscured. Prior to flight, the pilot-in command shall alert crew members of inoperative or missing equipment

Note.Para: Inoperative portable oxygen bottles or units, removed from a certified location or removed from the aircraft, are subject to dangerous goods regulations.
Medical equipment installed in the aircraft as part of an EMS operation is not considered part of the normal complement of equipment.

MaintenanceProcedure.Para: Ensure the inoperative portable oxygen bottles or units remain in a certified location and required distribution is maintained (AMM DDG 35-31-01)

ListItem.Para: Ensure inoperative or not properly serviced portable oxygen bottle/unit remains in a certified location until removed or serviced at the next suitable maintenance facility.
Ensure required distribution of serviceable units is maintained throughout the airplane.
Ensure the total quantity of oxygen in the operative bottles is adequate.
If maximum cruise altitude is FL250 or less, first aid oxygen is not required.

OperationNote.Para: Required Portable Oxygen Dispensing Units location:

DispatchConditionRemarks.Para: May be inoperative, damaged, or missing provided proper installation and operation is verified at each preflight.

OperationProcedure.Para: Verify the associated portable oxygen bottles or units are properly installed and operate correctly before each flight.
